service.name: ORAG tutorial P3 contextual retrieval fixture
service.purpose: This controlled corpus verifies server-owned contextual retrieval while preserving the P0 Basic parser and 800/120 recursive splitter.

components.description: ORAG validates requests, indexes verified documents, and records deterministic retrieval evidence for standard evaluation.
The API service listens on port 8080 and exposes project-scoped retrieval operations.
A private Pack is verified by checksum before any tutorial Live Run reads its content.
P0 uses the Basic parser with recursive chunk size 800 and overlap 120 text units.
P3 keeps the P0 parser and splitter unchanged, adding only server-owned contextual text.
The contextualizer uses a fixed prompt, bounded input, and fails the run rather than silently falling back.
Every candidate receives an isolated Knowledge Base derived from the immutable Pack declaration.
The evaluator runs the same declared dataset and profile for a compatible P0 and candidate pair.
Comparison reports measured index facts and never infers a quality improvement from contextualization alone.
A contextualized chunk stores non-empty contextual text that situates its content in the verified document.
The service persists the count of contextualized chunks and average deterministic context length.
Browser requests can select only a Pack-declared variant and provide an idempotency key.
Browser requests cannot supply prompts, model names, failure policies, storage coordinates, or context content.
The runtime definition fingerprint records the P3 contextualization contract and prompt version.
The comparison fingerprint contains shared environment facts but excludes the P3-only contextualization setting.
A completed P0 is the only allowed parent for the P3 contextual retrieval candidate.
P1 structured JSON parsing and P2 400/80 chunking remain independent tutorial candidates.
P3 contextual retrieval is experimental and its walkthrough proves auditable execution, not benchmark superiority.
The Console renders read-only contextualization facts returned by the API after the indexing stage.
The public documentation describes the strict-fail boundary and explains why no client setting can change it.
